# terrible_purestorage_flashblade_purefb_bucket_access (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) Name of Object Store bucket the policy applies to.

### Optional

- `actions` (String) List of permissions to grant. System-wide policy rules cannot be deleted or modified Currently only s3:GetObject is allowed
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `context` (String) Name of fleet member on which to perform the operation. This requires the array receiving the request is a member of a fleet and the context name to be a member of the same fleet.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `effect` (String) Allow S3 requests that match all of the *actions* item selected. Rules are additive.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `headers` (String) A list of headers that are permitted to be included in cross-origin requests to access a bucket. The only currently supported allowed header is '*'.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `methods` (String) A list of HTTP methods that are permitted for cross-origin requests to access a bucket. The only currently supported combination of allowed methods is all methods.
- `origins` (String) A list of origins (domains) that are permitted to make cross-origin requests to access a bucket. The only currently supported allowed origin is '*'.
- `policy_type` (String) Type of policy
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `principals` (Boolean) Defines if the rule will apply to all object store users regardless of their origin or principal.
- `resources` (String) The list of resources which this rule applies to. The only currently supported resource is all objects in a bucket to which the parent policy belongs.
- `rule` (String) Name of the rule in the Bucket Policy Required if *state* is *present*
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Create or delete policy or rule.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
